description: A beginner-friendly fitness companion built with MVUX and mock seeded data. An adaptive shell shows a NavigationView pane on wide screens (desktop/web) and a bottom TabBar on phones, behind a branded ExtendedSplashScreen. Home has a personalized greeting, today's workout with a Start button, weekly activity stats, recent results and quick tips. Includes a Plan page (scheduled workouts), a Progress page (history + milestones), a Profile page, and a guided Workout Session that opens full-screen from any workout. Clean, motivating UI on the Uno Toolkit Simple theme.

messages.content:
Generate a beginner-friendly fitness app with:
- mock state
- MVUX
- an adaptive shell with a bottom TabBar
- a clean, motivating Material UI

Home screen:
- a personalized greeting and motivation message
- today's workout card with a Start button
- weekly activity stats (minutes active, calories, completed vs goal days)
- recent workout results
- quick fitness tips

Navigation:
- Home, Plan (scheduled workouts), Progress (completed-workout history) and Profile pages
- a guided Workout Session flow

Include:
- full working code
- navigation between pages
- mock state
- seeded sample data (workouts, results, profile)
Here's your **FitStart** app! It's built and running in the preview.

suggestedPrompts: Add an exercise details page with navigation

messages.content:
Generate a beginner-friendly fitness app with:
- mock state
- MVUX
- an adaptive shell with a bottom TabBar
- a clean, motivating Material UI

Home screen:
- a personalized greeting and motivation message
- today's workout card with a Start button
- weekly activity stats (minutes active, calories, completed vs goal days)
- recent workout results
- quick fitness tips

Navigation:
- Home, Plan (scheduled workouts), Progress (completed-workout history) and Profile pages
- a guided Workout Session flow

Include:
- full working code
- navigation between pages
- mock state
- seeded sample data (workouts, results, profile)

I've created a complete Uno Platform app at FitBeginnerApp/FitBeginnerApp.csproj.

**Key files:**
- FitBeginnerApp/App.xaml (+ .cs) — Simple-theme resources, palette, styles, and the navigation route map.
- FitBeginnerApp/ThemeColors.xaml — the light/dark FitStart palette.
- FitBeginnerApp/Presentation/Shell.xaml (+ .cs), ShellModel.cs — the ExtendedSplashScreen host.
- FitBeginnerApp/Presentation/MainPage.xaml (+ .cs), MainModel.cs — the adaptive NavigationView/TabBar shell.
- FitBeginnerApp/Presentation/{Home,Plan,Progress,Profile,WorkoutSession}Page.xaml (+ .cs) and matching *Model.cs.
- FitBeginnerApp/Presentation/SharedTypes.cs — the domain records; Converters.cs — BoolToVisibility.
- FitBeginnerApp/Assets/Icons + Assets/Splash — the branded icon and splash (Uno.Resizetizer).

**Structure:**
- MVUX: each page has a partial-record *Model holding static seeded data (IReadOnlyList / scalars). No INotifyPropertyChanged, ObservableCollection-as-state, or RelayCommand.
- Uno.Extensions.Navigation: App.xaml.cs registers Shell (ExtendedSplashScreen) -> Main (the responsive shell, IsDefault) -> Home/Plan/Progress/Profile tabs, with WorkoutSession as a full-screen sibling of Main (DataViewMap<WorkoutSessionPage, WorkoutSessionModel, WorkoutEntry>).
- MainPage owns the chrome once: a NavigationView pane on wide layouts and a bottom TabBar on narrow ones, switched with {utu:Responsive}; the content region is filled by the active tab (uen:Region.Navigator="Visibility").
- Tapping a workout (Home's Start button, or a Plan card) navigates to WorkoutSession with the WorkoutEntry as Navigation.Data; the session opens full-screen and Back returns to the tab.
- Icons are inline PathIcon vector geometry (no FontIcon) so they render in Studio / Hot Design. The pane footer toggles light/dark via RequestedTheme.
- Domain records in SharedTypes.cs: ExerciseItem, WorkoutEntry, WorkoutResult, UserProfile.

The app is built and running. The user may ask me to modify it.
